docs.title: OpenMod4Africa – Documentation
docs.text:
Welcome to the OpenMod4Africa platform wiki.

OpenMod4Africa is a web-based visualization and modelling tool designed to support energy planning across Africa. It provides access to geospatial data layers, indicators, and calculation modules to help researchers, planners, and decision-makers explore energy access and infrastructure scenarios.

This wiki is the central place for platform documentation, including release notes, usage guides, and technical references.

docs.title: Contents
docs.text:
• Release Notes

This documentation is actively being built. More sections will be added over time.

docs.title: New Data Layers
docs.text:
• Global Horizontal Irradiance (GHI) with indicators
  • Night time lights
  • Major roads
  • Mini-hydro potential
  • Wind speed (50 m)
  • Electricity grid: high/medium/low/very-high voltage (existing & planned)
  • African transmission & distribution network
  • Facilities (OSM substations)
  • Workplaces
  • Points of interest
  • Travel time to cities
  • Settlement clusters with indicators
  • Minigrid localities (displayed on map, not clickable)

docs.title: New Calculation Modules
docs.text:
• Plan4res – integrated and available for energy system planning
  • OpenTEPES – transmission expansion planning
  • InfraFair – infrastructure cost allocation
  • ONSSET - [DEMO] least-cost electrification planning using geospatial and socio-economic data
  • EV-PV - simulates EV charging demand and its coverage with local PV generation
  • EV-PV helper - provides two independent tools that generate input data for the EV-PV CM

docs.title: Interface Improvements
docs.text:
• Layer attribution images displayed in the legend
  • Layer loading indicator while data is being fetched
  • Search bar on personal and shared uploaded layers
  • Expand/collapse panels for uploaded and shared layers
  • Improved display logic for "No results" and CM error messages
  • Formatted CM description text (supports rich text)

Go To Top
